checklist.description: CID computation is NOT inside a conditional block that checks for a signing parameter — it runs for every entry regardless of whether a signature/signingRequestId is provided
checklist.name: CID computed unconditionally

checklist.description: The code does NOT have a pattern like `if (signature) { computeCID(...) }` or `if (signingRequestId) { ... cid ... }` — the CID is always computed
checklist.name: No CID gated on signing

checklist.description: The implementation produces a CIDv1 (not CIDv0) content-addressed identifier
checklist.name: CIDv1 used

checklist.description: The signature/signing field is added to the entry separately from CID computation, and the function signature makes it optional
checklist.name: Signature is optional addition

checklist.description: The storage/insert step uses upsert logic (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, or equivalent) to handle duplicate entries with the same CID rather than failing with a unique constraint error
checklist.name: Idempotent upsert logic

checklist.description: The `example-usage.ts` shows calling the ingestion function WITHOUT a signature, demonstrating that entries can be created without signing
checklist.name: Example without signature

checklist.description: The stored entry record includes a `contentHash` (or equivalent) field populated from the computed CID for all entries
checklist.name: contentHash field populated

context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements content-addressed identifiers (CIDs) as a data integrity feature independent of signing, and handles idempotent upsert logic for deterministic identifiers.
